commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3bf8c56ef8bba01cc8b4db0cb35030ab5c27e373)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes write-time media cleanup from `saveMediaSource`, makes `cleanOldMedia` timer-owned and always recursive, updates `media.ttlHours` help text, and adjusts media cleanup tests.

PR surface: Source -6, Tests -46. Total -52 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection plus the fs-safe dependency contract show that current `saveMediaSource` can run a two-minute cleanup that descends into first-level media directories; the PR screenshots also show before/after file preservation behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow repair can preserve the shipped cleanup API while keeping the PR's intended removal of write-time pruning.

target.title: fix(media): stop pruning media on write; let the configured timer do it
target.body:
## Summary `saveMediaSource` ran an inline `cleanOldMedia(DEFAULT_TTL_MS, { recursive: false })` on every call. Two problems compounded: 1. `DEFAULT_TTL_MS` is **2 minutes**, hardcoded — it ignores the configurable `media.ttlHours` retention. 2. `cleanOldMedia` mapped `{ recursive: false }` to `pruneExpired({ maxDepth: 1, … })`. The `@openclaw/fs-safe` prune walker keys descent off `maxDepth` whenever it is set (`shouldDescend = maxDepth !== undefined ? depth < maxDepth : recursive`), so `recursive: false` still descended one level — into `media/inbound/`, `media/outbound/`, `media/tool-image-generation/`, etc. Net effect: preparing _any_ outgoing attachment silently mass-deleted unrelated attachments and AI-generated images older than 2 minutes across all first-level `media/` subdirectories, regardless of `media.ttlHours`. `media/` is canonical storage now (not the temporary Twilio scratch dir it was when this was added in `948ff7f`), so this is destructive legacy behavior. `pruneExpired` swallows per-file errors, so the deletions were unlogged. ## Fix Remove the inline cleanup from `saveMediaSource`. Media cleanup is now owned solely by the gateway maintenance timer (`startGatewayMaintenanceTimers`), which runs a full-tree recursive sweep using the operator-configured `media.ttlHours`. With `media.ttlHours` unset (default), no automatic cleanup runs — cleanup is opt-in. Because the timer is the only caller and always uses `{ recursive: true, pruneEmptyDirs: true }`, the now-dead shallow / `recursive: false` modes, the `CleanOldMediaOptions` type, and the `DEFAULT_TTL_MS` constant are removed. `cleanOldMedia(ttlMs)` now always does the full-tree recursive sweep. ## Behavior change - Writing media (`saveMediaSource` / `saveMediaBuffer` / `saveMediaStream`) never prunes. - Media is only pruned when `media.ttlHours` is set, via the maintenance timer. - `media.ttlHours` unset → no automatic media cleanup (help text updated accordingly). ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Preparing _any_ outgoing attachment silently mass-deleted unrelated attachments and AI-generated images older than 2 minutes across all first-level `media/` subdirectories, regardless of `media.ttlHours`. - Real environment tested: v2026.6.1 / Fixed branch - Exact steps or command run after this patch: - Ensure /media/inbound has a file >2 minutes old - Open web chat - Attach image - Request image generation - Check /media/inbound to see if file is deleted - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Screenshot of files now preserved - Observed result after fix: Files in /media are no longer deleted by attaching a file in web chat - What was not tested: Scheduled pruning (ttlHours) - Proof limitations or environment constraints: None - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Screenshot of files missing after attaching a file in web chat ## Verification - The `@openclaw/fs-safe@0.3.0` `pruneExpiredStoreEntries` descent logic was ported into a standalone Node reproduction and run against real temp directories, confirming the old `{ recursive: false }` deleted `media/inbound/` (the reported bug) and that `cleanOldMedia(ttlMs)` removes root-level and first-level expired files and does not follow symlinked directories. - The gateway timer's effective `pruneExpired` arguments are unchanged (`recursive: true, pruneEmptyDirs: true`, `maxDepth` undefined), so the recursive cleanup tests retain their original behavior. - Ran the related test suites locally — `pnpm test src/media/store.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts` — and all tests pass. Type-check (`pnpm tsgo`) is clean. - Built a Docker image from fix branch and installed it on a host. Confirmed files are no longer deleted when sending file attachment in web chat. ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No What is the highest-risk area? Media file cleanup How is that risk mitigated? Ran code to confirm file cleanup works as expected. ## Current review state What is the next action? What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?
Review state guidance Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.
target.author: lundog
